name: Potato Pie Crust
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Potatoes, mashed, unseasoned - at room temperature
1 tablespoon Water, or more, if needed
recipeInstructions:
1. Whip together the potatoes and oil using a wire whisk or fork.
2. Mix together the flour, baking powder, and salt.
3. Add the flour mixture to the potato mixture. Using a fork or your hands, blend the two mixtures together. Do not over work the dough. If it is too dry to hold together, gradually add water (a few drops at a time). You may not need any additional water if the mashed potatoes are fairly moist.
4. Roll the dough out onto a piece of wax paper. Sprinkle the counter or tabletop with a few drops of water to prevent slipping, and sprinkle the dough with flour to prevent sticking.
5. Pick up the dough by the edges of the paper and place it (paper side up) in a pie plate. Carefully peel off the paper.
6. Flute the edges of the pie crust, fill according to your recipe, and bake.
